en: For freedom Christ has set us free; stand firm therefore, and do not submit again to a yoke of slavery.
zh: 基督釋放了我們，為了要使我們得自由。所以你們要站立得穩，不要再被奴役的軛控制。

en: Look: I, Paul, say to you that if you accept circumcision, Christ will be of no advantage to you.
zh: 我保羅現在告訴你們，如果你們受割禮，基督對你們就毫無益處了。

en: I testify again to every man who accepts circumcision that he is obligated to keep the whole law.
zh: 我再對所有受割禮的人鄭重聲明：他有責任遵行全部的律法。

en: You are severed from Christ, you who would be justified by the law; you have fallen away from grace.
zh: 你們這些靠律法稱義的人，是和基督隔絕，從恩典中墜落了。

en: For through the Spirit, by faith, we ourselves eagerly wait for the hope of righteousness.
zh: 我們靠著聖靈，憑著信，熱切等候所盼望的義。

en: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision nor uncircumcision counts for anything, but only faith working through love.
zh: 因為在基督耶穌裡，受割禮或不受割禮，都沒有用處，唯有那藉著愛表達出來的信，才有用處。

en: You were running well. Who hindered you from obeying the truth?
zh: 你們向來跑得好，誰攔阻了你們，使你們不順從真理呢？

en: This persuasion is not from him who calls you.
zh: 這種勸誘不是出於那召你們的。

en: A little leaven leavens the whole lump.
zh: 一點麵酵能使全團麵發起來。

en: I have confidence in the Lord that you will take no other view than mine, and the one who is troubling you will bear the penalty, whoever he is.
zh: 我在主裡深信你們不會存別的意念；但那攪擾你們的，無論是誰，必定要受刑罰。

en: But if I, brothers, still preach circumcision, why am I still being persecuted? In that case the offense of the cross has been removed.
zh: 弟兄們，如果我仍舊傳割禮，我為甚麼還受迫害呢？若是這樣，十字架絆倒人的地方就沒有了。

en: I wish those who unsettle you would emasculate themselves!
zh: 我恨不得那些擾亂你們的人把自己閹割了！

en: For you were called to freedom, brothers. Only do not use your freedom as an opportunity for the flesh, but through love serve one another.
zh: 弟兄們，你們蒙召得了自由；只是不可把這自由當作放縱情慾的機會，總要憑著愛心互相服事。

en: For the whole law is fulfilled in one word: "You shall love your neighbor as yourself."
zh: 因為全部的律法，都在“愛人如己”這一句話裡面成全了。

en: But if you bite and devour one another, watch out that you are not consumed by one another.
zh: 你們要謹慎，如果相咬相吞，恐怕彼此都要毀滅了。

en: But I say, walk by the Spirit, and you will not gratify the desires of the flesh.
zh: 我是說，你們應當順著聖靈行事，這樣就一定不會去滿足肉體的私慾了。

en: For the desires of the flesh are against the Spirit, and the desires of the Spirit are against the flesh, for these are opposed to each other, to keep you from doing the things you want to do.
zh: 因為肉體的私慾和聖靈敵對，聖靈也和肉體敵對；這兩樣互相敵對，使你們不能作自己願意作的。

en: But if you are led by the Spirit, you are not under the law.
zh: 但你們若被聖靈引導，就不在律法以下了。

en: Now the works of the flesh are evident: sexual immorality, impurity, sensuality,
zh: 肉體所行的都是顯而易見的，就如淫亂、污穢、邪蕩、

en: idolatry, sorcery, enmity, strife, jealousy, fits of anger, rivalries, dissensions, divisions,
zh: 拜偶像、行邪術、仇恨、爭競、忌恨、忿怒、自私、分黨、結派、

en: envy, drunkenness, orgies, and things like these. I warn you, as I warned you before, that those who do such things will not inherit the kingdom of God.
zh: 嫉妒、醉酒、荒宴，和類似的事。我從前早就告訴過你們，現在又事先告訴你們：行這些事的人，必定不能承受 神的國。

en: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness,
zh: 但聖靈的果子是仁愛、喜樂、平安、忍耐、恩慈、良善、信實、

en: gentleness, self-control; against such things there is no law.
zh: 溫柔、節制；這樣的事，是沒有律法禁止的。

en: And those who belong to Christ Jesus have crucified the flesh with its passions and desires.
zh: 屬基督耶穌的人，是已經把肉體和邪情私慾都釘在十字架上了。

en: If we live by the Spirit, let us also walk by the Spirit.
zh: 如果我們靠聖靈活著，就應該順著聖靈行事。

en: Let us not become conceited, provoking one another, envying one another.
zh: 我們不可貪圖虛榮，彼此觸怒，互相嫉妒。
